title: Ksp Precipitation Predictor
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Mix two ionic solutions (with optional dilution inputs) to compute Q vs Ksp and visualize whether a precipitate forms.

learningObjectives: Calculate the ion product Q for sparingly soluble salts and compare it to Ksp.
Account for dilution when solutions are mixed to predict precipitation outcomes.

keyQuestions: Is Q greater than, less than, or equal to Ksp for the given mixture?
How does dilution change [ion] after mixing two solutions?
commonMisconceptions: Precipitation depends only on Ksp, not the actual concentrations.
Volume changes do not affect molarity when solutions mix.
assessmentOpportunities: Learners submit Q vs Ksp calculations for assigned salts.
Students explain log-bar positions and verdict text for different scenarios.
